commentsOrObservations: The session began by asking the students if they could now introduce them in ways more than their names followed by an activity and discussion afterwards.
activity: The activity included a three word introduction, which encouraged them to think about themselves in three words or how they would like to "tag" them which would be understood just by a word. Followed by writing their weaknesses or things they would like to get better at, and they were discussed altoghether.
taskAssignedOrReflection: The students were able to understand and write their three word introduction and about their weaknesses with the help of the facilitator and were excited to share and discuss about it in the session.
interventionForEducators: The educators were encouraged to talk to the students and emphasize on the importance of wanting to work on themselves and identify their best qualities which helps them use as their strength.

commentsOrObservations: The session started with the facilitator encouraging the students to sit for a minute and think on the basis of prompts given to them. After which an activity was taken up in which the students had to write those things and further they were discussed. In case of any misunderstanding, it was cleared up by the facilitator.
activity: The activity included the students drawing their hand print on a piece of paper and for each finger a different prompt was given to them to write.
taskAssignedOrReflection: After the activity few of the students were able to fill all the prompts but a few needed assistance to understand what they need to write. After examples and discussion with the facilitator, the students were able to complete most of the prompts.
interventionForEducators: The educators were told to keep the students from thinking only in terms of their names and move beyond in their understanding about themselves.
followUpActivity: The students were encouraged to think about themselves in ways that describes them other than their name and their school ID cards. They were also asked to complete the left out prompts which they were not able to complete the next time session happens.

commentsOrObservations:
Students participated enthusiastically and shared positive qualities confidently
Demonstrated appreciation and emotional connection toward family members
activity: Students drew their family members and wrote one positive attribute about each person.
taskAssignedOrReflection: Express appreciation toward a family member at home.
followUpActivity: Discussion on kindness and appreciation in relationships.

commentsOrObservations: The session introduced mindfulness-based coping tools and encouraged students to use visualization during stressful situations.
activity: Introduction to visualization techniques and incorporating them into personal stress-coping toolkits.
taskAssignedOrReflection: Practice visualization strategies during moments of stress or worry.
followUpActivity: Guided visualization practice and discussion on coping experiences.
